essence: Determines the cause and origin of fires through forensic investigation, evidence analysis, and scene examination.

identity.title: Fire Investigation Officer (Fire Investigator)
identity.summary: Examines fire scenes, identifies ignition sources, analyses burn patterns, collects evidence, and works with police and insurers to determine how fires started.

salary.notes: Senior investigators, forensic specialists, and complex‑incident leads can exceed £55k-£65k+.

qualifications: Fire investigation or forensic investigation training
Knowledge of fire behaviour, ignition sources, and burn patterns
Understanding of evidence handling and scene preservation
Ability to write detailed forensic reports and give expert testimony

lifestyle.description: Fire investigation officers attend fire scenes to determine how and why a fire started. They analyse burn patterns, identify ignition sources, collect evidence, and collaborate with police, insurers, and forensic teams. The role suits individuals who enjoy investigative work, forensic detail, and contributing to public safety and justice.

habits: Preserves and documents fire scenes carefully
Analyses burn patterns and material behaviour
Collects and handles evidence according to forensic standards
Works closely with police, insurers, and fire crews
